title: galium ciliare subsp. terminale
intro: Galium ciliare subsp. terminale, also known as the terminal bee orchid, is a rare and endangered plant species within the Galium genus, characterized by its unique, orchid-like appearance. The medicinal properties of Galium ciliare subsp. terminale are attributed to its alkaloid content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. This plant is believed to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Galium ciliare subsp. terminale can be found in traditional medicine, often in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its availability and usage may vary due to conservation concerns and limited research.
